updates.title: Anthropic Extends Claude Fable 5 Free Access Through July 19 — Claude Code Rate Limits Also Stay Elevated
updates.summary: Anthropic extended free access to Claude Fable 5 for Pro, Max, Team, and eligible Enterprise subscribers through July 19, 2026 at 11:59:59 PM PT, marking the third extension since the original June 22 cutoff date (previously pushed to July 7, then July 12). Subscribers can use Fable 5 for up to 50% of their weekly usage limits at no extra cost, though it draws from the same pool as other models. Claude Code's 50% higher weekly rate limits are also extended through July 19; after that date all Fable 5 usage requires prepaid usage credits at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed by BleepingComputer, Dataconomy, Forbes (July 12–13), TechTimes (July 12), and multiple other independent tech publications citing Anthropic's official support document. Pricing and deadline details are consistent across all sources. The 'third extension' count is verified against prior July 7 and July 12 deadlines. High confidence.

updates.title: Claude Code is the best productivity tool I've installed this year, and you don't need to write a single line of code to use it
updates.summary: XDA writer argues that Claude Code is the standout productivity tool of 2026 for non-developers, explaining that pointing it at a folder lets it treat the entire directory as a system — noticing file relationships, catching inconsistencies, and understanding structural conventions without any coding from the user. The article traces the author's journey from dismissing Claude Code as a developer tool to relying on it daily for productivity workflows. The piece highlights how Claude Code's ability to hold a whole folder in context differentiates it from simpler chatbot interactions.
updates.accuracy_notes: Published article from a reputable tech outlet; reflects first-hand user experience rather than official Anthropic data. Claims about Claude Code's folder-awareness and file-relationship understanding align with documented Claude Code capabilities. Note: this is an opinion/how-to piece, so the 'best tool' framing is subjective. Verify Claude Code's current Pro plan inclusion status given recent A/B test reports.
